goal_summary: Get Cassandra to agree to a specific call time with Andre so we can confirm details for her May 30 rodis wedding and send the right package link.
lead_state_summary: Cassandra Jamieson just requested catering via Facebook Ads (event type shows “RODS WEDDING & FACELESS” and “Graduation party” metadata) with event date May 30. She asked for packages/meals description and specifically requested a link to packages (inbound SMS 17:45 and 17:48). Andre already asked for the best time of day to give her a call (outbound SMS 17:27), and Cassandra replied “Got it. Thanks” at 17:48. An email was sent 18:50 titled “Understanding Our Catering Service Types,” but she hasn’t booked a call yet.
known_facts: Lead is tagged as “RODS WEDDING & FACELESS” and “VIDEO RODS WEDDING & FACELESS.”
Cassandra asked for packages/meal description (inbound SMS at 17:45).
Cassandra asked for a link to packages (inbound SMS at 17:29).
Andre sent an email titled “Understanding Our Catering Service Types” at 18:50.
Andre asked for the best time of day to give Cassandra a call (outbound SMS at 17:27).
Cassandra responded “Got it. Thanks” (inbound SMS at 17:48).
unknowns: Her event location/city and venue details for May 30.
Guest count and serving style (plated, buffet, family-style).
Any dietary restrictions or preferred cuisine/meal types.
Whether she wants a wedding package specifically vs general catering for the May 30 event.
A specific time window today/tomorrow that works for a call with Andre.
best_next_question: For May 30, about how many guests are you planning for, and what time today or tomorrow is best for Andre to call you?

days.objective: Lock a specific call time by answering her package-link request and proposing two concrete windows for Andre.

required_actions.intent: Send package-link + propose 2 call time options for Andre
required_actions.draft_seed:
Cassandra, thanks for replying. For your May 30 event, can Andre call you today or tomorrow?
1) today 4:30–6:00 PM
2) tomorrow 10:00–12:00 PM
Also—do you want our package link for wedding-style service or general catering? Reply 1 or 2.
required_actions.notes: SMS is appropriate since her last inbound was an SMS and she asked for packages.

days.objective: If she didn’t book Day 1, clarify guest count + dietary needs to make the call immediately useful.

required_actions.intent: Ask 2 quick clarifying questions; offer to schedule Andre call
required_actions.draft_seed:
Subject: Quick details for May 30 (then Andre can confirm packages)

Hi Cassandra,

Before Andre calls, can you share:
1) roughly how many guests?
2) any dietary restrictions or must-have meal types?

If it’s easier, tell me which works: today after 5 PM, or tomorrow late morning, and we’ll get Andre on the phone.
required_actions.notes: This answers her “packages/meals” question without sending repetitive info since email was already sent today.

days.objective: Use a short SMS to confirm the phone call purpose and reduce friction: confirm event type and preferred service style.

required_actions.intent: Confirm event type + service style; ask for call confirmation
required_actions.draft_seed:
Cassandra—quick one so Andre sends the right options: for May 30, is this wedding catering (RODS) or a different celebration?
Also, would you prefer buffet or plated/family-style?
Want Andre to call at 6:00 PM tonight or 11:00 AM tomorrow?
required_actions.notes: Keeps the thread conversational; asks for booking with two options.

days.objective: If no response, send a calendar-style scheduling task that asks Andre to call at a specific time (with user confirming).

required_actions.intent: Call Cassandra Jamieson to schedule on Andre's calendar: propose 20-min call at 5:00 PM
required_actions.draft_seed: Task for Andre: Offer a 20-minute call to Cassandra on May 9 at 5:00 PM (lead-local) to confirm guest count, service style, and finalize the right package link.
required_actions.notes: Agent cannot call directly; task is Andre outreach once Cassandra agrees via message.

required_actions.intent: Ask Cassandra to confirm the proposed time and reply with guest count
required_actions.draft_seed:
Cassandra, does a 20-minute call with Andre work at 5:00 PM? Reply “yes” or suggest another time.
And what guest count are you planning for May 30?
required_actions.notes: Second touch same day uses SMS after the task seed; distinct intent (time confirmation + guest count).

days.objective: Address objections implicitly by asking what matters most (price, variety, timing) and offer Andre call accordingly.

required_actions.intent: NEPQ-style question: what matters most for her packages so Andre can tailor
required_actions.draft_seed:
Subject: What matters most for your May 30 catering?

Hi Cassandra,

When you look at packages, what matters most for you?
- staying within a budget
- lots of menu variety
- dietary-friendly options
- service style (buffet vs plated)

If you tell me which one, I’ll have Andre call with the best-matching options. What time is best for that call?
required_actions.notes: Prepares for a scheduled call by narrowing decision criteria.

days.objective: If still not scheduled, send a final scheduling SMS with two near-term windows and a direct “reply with X” CTA.

required_actions.intent: Final scheduling attempt: two windows + quick menu preference
required_actions.draft_seed:
Cassandra, to make this easy: Andre can call either tomorrow at 12:30 PM or 3:30 PM. Which time should I lock in?
Also, are you leaning more toward chicken/steak/seafood for the main?
required_actions.notes: Short and specific; aims to get a scheduling confirmation.

days.objective: Convert intent into action: ask for a direct yes/no to a scheduled call and remind her of the May 30 target date.

required_actions.intent: Confirm call or ask to pause politely
required_actions.draft_seed:
Cassandra—do you want Andre to call and finalize your May 30 package details?
Reply “call me” and pick a time (choose 1: 10–12 or 2: 4–6), or reply “not now.”
required_actions.notes: Includes an explicit choice to encourage response.

stop_conditions.trigger: Inbound message contains stop/opt-out keywords (e.g., “stop”, “remove me”, “not interested”)
Lead schedules a phone call with Andre (confirmed time in SMS/email response)
